g.data-narration: Welcome to Digital Logic Gates. This comprehensive diagram will guide you through every essential gate and its applications.
g.text: Fundamental Building Blocks of Digital Circuits | CSE 2022

g.data-narration: Logic gates are physical implementations of Boolean operations - electronic circuits that process binary inputs to produce binary outputs.
g.text: Electronic circuits with binary inputs → single binary output
Building blocks for ALL digital systems

g.data-narration: Understanding gates is essential - they connect Boolean algebra to physical hardware and form the foundation of all digital devices.
g.text: Bridge between theory (Boolean algebra) and hardware
Every digital device uses billions of gates
Essential for processor design, memory circuits, IoT devices

g.data-narration: The AND gate produces output 1 only when ALL inputs are 1. Its Boolean expression is Y = A · B, implemented with a D-shaped symbol.
g.text: Output = 1 only when ALL inputs are 1

g.data-narration: The OR gate outputs 1 when at least ONE input is 1. Expression: Y = A + B, with a curved input side and pointed output symbol.
g.text: Output = 1 when at least ONE input is 1

g.data-narration: The NOT gate inverts the input - output is the complement. Symbol: triangle with bubble. Expression: Y = A' (A-bar).
g.text: Inverts the input - output is the complement

g.data-narration: NAND gate = NOT of AND. Output is 0 only when ALL inputs are 1. It's a universal gate - can implement ANY Boolean function.
g.text: NOT of AND - Output is 0 only when ALL inputs are 1

g.data-narration: NOR gate = NOT of OR. Output is 1 ONLY when ALL inputs are 0. Like NAND, it's also a universal gate for any Boolean function.
g.text: NOT of OR - Output is 1 ONLY when ALL inputs are 0

g.data-narration: XOR (Exclusive OR) outputs 1 when inputs are DIFFERENT. Expression: Y = A'B + AB'. Essential for adders and comparators.
g.text: Output is 1 when inputs are DIFFERENT

g.data-narration: XNOR outputs 1 when inputs are SAME (both 0 or both 1). Expression: Y = A ⊙ B = AB + A'B'. Called equivalence gate.
g.text: Output is 1 when inputs are SAME (both 0 or both 1)

g.data-narration: Gate characteristics include fan-in (inputs accepted), fan-out (gates driven), and propagation delay (signal travel time in nanoseconds).
Common IC families include 74xx (TTL) and 74HCxx/74ACxx (CMOS). Each chip packages multiple gates - see common gate ICs below.
Key takeaways: 7 main gates (AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR) form the foundation of all digital systems.
g.text: AND, OR, NOT, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR
NAND and NOR can implement ANY Boolean function

g.data-narration: After mastering gates, you're ready for combinational circuits, sequential circuits, K-map minimization, and timing analysis.
